index.name: Nifty Bank
index.description: The Nifty Bank Index tracks 12 most liquid and large-cap Indian banking stocks on NSE. Serves as benchmark for banking ETFs and F&O derivatives. Heavily influenced by RBI monetary policy, credit growth cycles, and NPA trends. One of the most actively traded indices globally — Nifty Bank weekly options are among the world's highest volume derivative contracts.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Post-merger with HDFC Ltd, a financial colossus with ₹25 lakh crore in assets. India's largest private bank by market cap. Processes 1 in 5 retail credit card transactions in India. 8,700+ branches and 21,000+ ATMs.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Aggressive growth under CEO Sandeep Bakhshi. Industry-best ROE of 18%+ among large private banks. iMobile Pay has 10M+ users. Strong in all segments — retail mortgages, corporate, SME and international banking.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium banking franchise with exceptional asset quality — GNPA below 2%. Converted from NBFC in 2003. Burgundy Wealth management for HNIs and 811 zero-balance digital account are standout differentiated products.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank with ₹55 lakh crore in assets, 22,000+ branches and 65,000+ ATMs. YONO app has 40M+ registered users. Dominant in MSME, agri and government-backed lending. Consistent dividend payer.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Third-largest private bank with 10,000+ touch points. Citibank India acquisition in 2023 strengthened wealth management (AUM doubled) and credit cards (now top 3 issuer). Digital-first under CEO Amitabh Chaudhry.

holdings.name: Bank of Baroda
holdings.description: Second-largest PSU bank post Vijaya Bank and Dena Bank merger in 2019. International presence in 100+ countries. Post-NPA cleanup showing sustainable ROE improvement and strong loan growth.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: Mid-size private bank with stronghold in vehicle finance (CV and 2W loans accounting for 30% book) and microfinance. Nexxt credit card. Strong corporate banking franchise and growing retail deposit franchise.

holdings.name: Punjab National Bank
holdings.description: India's second-largest PSU bank by branches, founded in 1894 in Lahore. Post-NPA cleanup showing sustainable profitability improvement. Government ownership provides stability. Strong in MSME, agri and priority sector lending.

holdings.name: Federal Bank
holdings.description: Kerala-headquartered private bank with 1,400+ branches. Strong NRI deposit franchise (Kerala diaspora in Gulf). Fintech partnerships (Google Pay, Paytm) drive digital transactions. Consistent performer with improving ROA.

holdings.name: IDFC First Bank
holdings.description: Transformed from an infrastructure NBFC to a full-service retail bank under MD V. Vaidyanathan. Rapid deposit mobilisation, strong microfinance and vehicle finance franchises. Merged with IDFC Ltd creating a clean balance sheet.

index.name: Nifty Private Bank Index
index.description: Tracks only private sector banks within the Nifty Banking universe. Excludes PSU banks (SBI, PNB, BOB). Private banks historically deliver higher ROE, better asset quality, and superior earnings growth due to technology investment and retail banking penetration.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: India's largest private bank post HDFC merger. ₹25 trillion balance sheet. Stable asset quality with GNPA ~1.3%. Largest home loan book via HDFC Ltd integration. Digital: 90M+ customers on mobile banking.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Second-largest private bank. Transformation under CEO Sandeep Bakhshi — NIMs expanded, opex controlled, ROE 17%+. Loan book well-diversified across retail, SME, and corporate. iMobile Pay has 9M+ users.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium private bank with one of the industry's best asset quality records. GNPA consistently below 2%. Uday Kotak's franchise known for conservative underwriting. Kotak 811 digital bank has 4M+ accounts.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Third-largest private bank. Citibank India retail portfolio acquisition added 3M premium credit card customers. Ultra FD and premium banking products. ROE improving trajectory with credit cost normalisation.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: Wholesale and commercial banking with Bharat Financial Inclusion (microfinance) subsidiary. Vehicle financing market leader. Consumer finance growing rapidly. Mid-size bank with high NIM profile.

holdings.name: Federal Bank
holdings.description: Kerala-headquartered private bank with pan-India ambitions. NRI remittances from Gulf a key low-cost float source. Well-capitalized with strong retail and SME loan growth. Strong South India brand.

holdings.name: IDFC First Bank
holdings.description: Transformed from infrastructure NBFC to retail bank under V. Vaidyanathan. Zero-fee savings account and high-interest FD strategy. Retail loan growth, credit card and personal loan momentum strong.

holdings.name: Bandhan Bank
holdings.description: Microfinance-turned-bank serving rural and semi-urban India. West Bengal and Northeast concentration risk. Highest NIM in banking (~7%). Diversifying into housing finance and SME banking.

holdings.name: RBL Bank
holdings.description: Specialty banking in credit cards (co-branded with Bajaj Finance) and microfinance. Niche SME banking franchise. Turnaround story under new management post CEO transition in 2022.

holdings.name: Yes Bank
holdings.description: Reconstructed bank post 2020 RBI intervention. Now owned by SBI (26%) + consortium of banks. Clean balance sheet, improving operational metrics. Small weighting due to reduced free-float market cap.
